description:
You are a database administrator who has been asked to formalise the way ad-hoc SQL files are “optimised” (in this context, simply upper-casing all keywords and normalising extra spaces).  
    A colleague left you an initial scratch directory, but nothing is automated or reproducible yet.  
    Solve the following, strictly in the order given:

    1.  Create a dedicated, *user-local* Python virtual environment at  
        /home/user/.venvs/opt_queries  

        • The directory must be produced by a standard venv-style tool (for example, “python3 -m venv”).  
        • It must contain its own `bin/pip`, `bin/python`, etc., as usual for venvs.  

    2.  Inside that environment install exactly version 0.4.4 of the PyPI package “sqlparse”.  
        No other third-party package is required.  

    3.  Still inside the virtual environment, create a dependency lock file called  
        /home/user/.venvs/opt_queries/requirements.txt  

        • The file must be created by a **pip freeze** style command.  
        • The automated tester will only look for the literal line  
          `sqlparse==0.4.4`  
          so the presence or order of `pip`, `setuptools` etc. will not be tested, but they may appear.  

    4.  A colleague already placed a raw SQL file here (this file already exists when you start):  
        /home/user/db_queries/raw_queries.sql  

        4.1  First make a *verbatim* backup copy of the file in the **same directory** with the exact name  
              /home/user/db_queries/raw_queries_backup.sql  

    5.  Write a Python script named  
        /home/user/db_queries/optimize_queries.py  

        The script must:  
        • Be runnable with the venv’s `python`.  
        • Read `/home/user/db_queries/raw_queries.sql`.  
        • Use `sqlparse` to produce a *single-line* version of each query, with SQL keywords upper-cased and  
          a space inserted around comparison operators (e.g. “salary>50000” → “salary > 50000”).  
        • Write the transformed queries, one per line in the same order, to  
          /home/user/db_queries/optimized_queries.sql  

        The expected final contents of optimized_queries.sql (including the terminating newline) **must be exactly**:  
            SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000;  
            SELECT name, department FROM employees WHERE department = 'Sales';  

    6.  After the script runs successfully, create a plain-text log file named  
        /home/user/db_queries/optimization.log  

        It must contain exactly the five lines below (each followed by a newline, no extra spacing):  
            OPTIMIZATION SESSION START  
            Input file: raw_queries.sql  
            Output file: optimized_queries.sql  
            Queries optimized: 2  
            OPTIMIZATION SESSION END  

    7.  Verify by printing the following to the terminal:  
        a) the first two lines of /home/user/.venvs/opt_queries/requirements.txt  
        b) the full contents of /home/user/db_queries/optimized_queries.sql  
        c) the full contents of /home/user/db_queries/optimization.log  

    The automated grader will check:  
        • the existence of the virtual environment directory with its standard sub-structure;  
        • that `sqlparse==0.4.4` appears in requirements.txt;  
        • that optimized_queries.sql and optimization.log are byte-for-byte identical to the required content;  
        • that the backup file is present and identical to the original raw SQL.  
    No root privileges are needed; everything must be achievable from an ordinary user shell in /home/user.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file before the agent starts -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/db_queries/raw_queries.sql">
SELECT * from employees where salary>50000;
select name, department from employees where department='Sales';
</file>
    </initial_files>

    <!-- Files that must exist after successful completion -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/db_queries/optimized_queries.sql">
SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000;
SELECT name, department FROM employees WHERE department = 'Sales';
</file>

        <file path="/home/user/db_queries/optimization.log">
OPTIMIZATION SESSION START
Input file: raw_queries.sql
Output file: optimized_queries.sql
Queries optimized: 2
OPTIMIZATION SESSION END
</file>
    </expected_files>

    <!-- Minimal requirement line the grader will look for -->
    <requirements_line>sqlparse==0.4.4</requirements_line>

    <!-- Directory that must be created -->
    <venv_dir>/home/user/.venvs/opt_queries</venv_dir>
